image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "shown in the figure"
the task references a system diagram and a zero-sequence network, which are inherently visual/domain-diagram objects
multi_step: question_text references both a "2-bus system" and a "corresponding zero-sequence network"
the task asks to infer how transformers T1 and T2 are connected from the relationship between those two representations
this likely requires at least diagram interpretation plus power-system reasoning, not simple lookup from text alone
multiple-choice format may cap complexity, so score is moderate rather than extremely high
verifiability: answer_text is a fixed discrete label: "option B"
image asset exists, so downstream evaluators can in principle compare against the visual source

risk_flags: core semantics are image-dependent and not recoverable from text alone

decision_reasons: Question, image, and answer assets are present, so the sample retains core recoverable semantics and should stay in the candidate pool.
The problem is highly image-dependent, and the scoring record notes that the core semantics are not recoverable from text alone.
The risk flag indicates the choice texts are placeholders only, which reduces downstream usability and makes the answer label less informative without follow-up reconstruction.
Because the sample is still potentially usable but has meaningful completeness/interpretability risk, low_priority is more appropriate than keep.
